title: Course Schedule (Detect Cycle)
prompt:
Given numCourses courses labeled from 0 to numCourses - 1 and a list of prerequisite pairs, return true if you can finish all courses.

Each pair [a, b] means you must take course b before course a.

Return false if there is a cycle in the prerequisite graph.
constraintQuestions: Are all courses labeled 0 to numCourses - 1? (Yes.)
Are there cycles in the prerequisites? (Possibly.)
Should we return false if any cycle exists? (Yes.)

walkthrough.title: Build graph + indegree
walkthrough.body:
Create an adjacency list of outgoing edges and an indegree array.
For each pair [a, b], add edge b -> a and increment indegree[a].

walkthrough.title: Topological sort
walkthrough.body:
Push all nodes with indegree 0 into a stack/queue.
Pop nodes, count them as processed, and reduce indegree of neighbors.
If a neighbor reaches 0, push it.
If processed count equals numCourses, there is no cycle.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#
Python
codeSnippets.body:
from collections import deque


def canFinish(numCourses, prerequisites):
    graph = [[] for _ in range(numCourses)]
    indegree = [0] * numCourses

    for course, pre in prerequisites:
        graph[pre].append(course)
        indegree[course] += 1

    q = deque(i for i in range(numCourses) if indegree[i] == 0)
    taken = 0

    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        taken += 1
        for nxt in graph[cur]:
            indegree[nxt] -= 1
            if indegree[nxt] == 0:
                q.append(nxt)

    return taken == numCourses

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(V + E) for vertices and edges.
Space: O(V + E) for adjacency list and indegree.

tests.name: Cycle created by flipping one edge
